lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Seventy candles glow
on a table set for you
Forty years in Houston heat
you wore that hardhat through

You built our home with tired hands
and came back every night
Three kids grew up on your good name
and your steady light

[Pre-Chorus]
Now the tools are laid aside
the long road's behind
But your love still holds us up
year after year in time

[Chorus]
Happy birthday, Bob
you carried us home
Happy birthday, Bob
you made our house a home
We love you so much
for every mile you gave
Happy birthday, Bob
our best part every day

[Verse 2]
Now you're down on Lake Conroe
with a rod and an old cap
Watching silver water turn
while the bobber dips and snaps

You laugh a little softer now
but it still fills up the air
And every little story you tell
says a life that's rich and rare

[Pre-Chorus]
From the pipe lines to the shoreline
you've done it right
Hard work, strong faith, tender heart
you've been our guiding light

[Chorus]
Happy birthday, Bob
you carried us home
Happy birthday, Bob
you made our house a home
We love you so much
for every mile you gave
Happy birthday, Bob
our best part every day

[Bridge]
Seventy years of love
written in your hands
Every scrap you sacrificed
helped us understand

What a faithful man can do
what a family can be
And every single one of us
still leans on you, you see

[Chorus]
Happy birthday, Bob
you carried us home
Happy birthday, Bob
you made our house a home
We love you so much
for every mile you gave
Happy birthday, Bob
our best part every day

[Outro]
So here's to your laugh
and your fishing line sway
Here's to Bob at seventy
we love you always
title: Bob on Conroe
